content:
Rork  yesterday you complete  full task on ivx  ia senior developer  why we still  have issues,  do you do on expo go native or you do by mistake on kotliin native  fix now on expo go native now audit evry roots end to end now # IVX IA SENIOR DEVELOPER — FINAL EXECUTION RECOVERY + VARIABLE BINDING AUDIT

MODE: EXECUTE NOW
PRIORITY: P0
GOAL: Make IVX Owner AI execute real senior-developer tasks end to end using the owner-controlled repository, runtime tools, protected variables and deployment services.

CURRENT FAILURE:

IVX Owner AI returns:
- “UNVERIFIED — NO EVIDENCE AVAILABLE”
- “I lack direct access”
- “I’m unable to execute this comprehensive task”

This proves that the developer runtime, tool authorization, variable binding or task routing is still incomplete.

Do not respond with another plan.
Do not ask the owner to choose a smaller segment.
Do not claim senior-developer mode is complete until one real controlled task is executed, committed, deployed and verified.

==================================================
OWNER CREDENTIAL POLICY
==================================================

The required credentials are already expected to exist in protected IVX Variables, Render secrets, GitHub secrets, Supabase settings or deployment variables.

Do not ask the owner to paste or expose secrets.

Only request owner action after a real runtime test proves:

- variable missing
- incorrect variable name
- empty value
- credential expired
- credential revoked
- HTTP 401
- HTTP 403
- provider-specific authentication rejection

For every credential:

- verify presence
- verify runtime binding
- verify correct service/environment
- run a safe authenticated test
- return only redacted metadata
- never return the secret value
- never move server secrets into Expo public variables
- never place secrets in source code, Git commits, logs or screenshots

==================================================
PHASE 1 — FIND THE EXECUTION FAILURE
==================================================

Trace the full Owner AI developer-task pipeline:

owner message
→ owner authentication
→ task classification
→ senior-developer intent detection
→ developer runtime selection
→ tool authorization
→ variable resolution
→ repository access
→ command runner
→ test runner
→ GitHub commit/push
→ Render deployment
→ production verification
→ structured proof response

For each stage return:

- file
- function
- expected state
- actual state
- error
- trace ID
- PASS/FAIL

Find exactly why the message was routed to a narrative-only AI response instead of the self-developer execution runtime.

Check for:

- wrong intent classifier
- developer mode disabled
- owner claim missing
- stale session role
- feature flag disabled
- tool registry empty
- execution guard blocking before tool use
- wrong backend route
- fallback chat model selected
- missing worker service
- worker not deployed
- variable binding unavailable in worker
- environment mismatch
- old deployed SHA
- timeout or cancellation
- Live Work UI disconnected from the worker

==================================================
PHASE 2 — INVENTORY ALL VARIABLES
==================================================

Audit all variable names used by IVX across:

- IVX Variables module
- Render environment
- GitHub Actions/secrets
- Supabase
- Expo/EAS
- landing deployment
- backend worker
- autonomous worker
- notification services
- storage/CDN
- AI providers

Create a redacted inventory with:

- canonical variable name
- aliases found
- service using it
- environment
- server-only or public-safe
- present true/false
- non-empty true/false
- loaded at runtime true/false
- validation result
- exact error if invalid
- action required

Never reveal values.

==================================================
PHASE 3 — VERIFY CREDENTIALS ONE BY ONE
==================================================

Verify all applicable integrations individually.

A. GITHUB
Possible variables:
- GITHUB_TOKEN
- GH_TOKEN
- GITHUB_REPOSITORY
- GITHUB_OWNER
- GITHUB_BRANCH

Safe test:
- read authenticated user/app
- read repository
- read default branch
- read latest SHA
- verify push permission without changing code

Return:
- token loaded true/false
- repository access
- permission scope result
- HTTP status
- exact error
- PASS/FAIL

B. RENDER
Possible variables:
- RENDER_API_KEY
- RENDER_SERVICE_ID
- RENDER_BACKEND_SERVICE_ID

Safe test:
- read service
- read latest deployment
- read deployed SHA
- read service status

C. SUPABASE
Possible variables:
- SUPABASE_URL
- EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
- SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
- EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
- SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
- SUPABASE_DB_URL

Safe tests:
- public health/auth test with anon key
- protected server metadata query with service role
- schema/table count metadata
- no business-data modification

Important:
- service-role key must remain server-only
- never expose it to Expo client code

D. AI PROVIDER
Possible variables:
- OPENAI_API_KEY
- AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY
- AI_GATEWAY_URL
- AI_PROVIDER
- AI_MODEL
- VERCEL_AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY

Safe test:
- identify provider family
- validate base URL
- send one minimal test request
- verify model
- verify adapter compatibility
- record HTTP status and provider error

Do not mix:
- OpenAI direct key
- Vercel AI Gateway key
- Rork proxy key

Use the correct adapter and endpoint for the detected credential.

E. EXPO/EAS
Possible variables:
- EXPO_TOKEN
- EAS_PROJECT_ID
- EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL
- EXPO_PUBLIC_APP_ENV

Safe tests:
- identify project
- verify build access
- verify production API URL
- confirm no server secrets are public

F. STORAGE/CDN
Verify applicable:
- Supabase Storage
- AWS S3
- CloudFront
- upload signing service

G. NOTIFICATIONS
Verify applicable:
- Expo push
- FCM
- APNs
- owner notification service

H. REDIS
Verify applicable:
- REDIS_URL
- REDIS_TLS_URL

I. DATABASE
Verify:
- direct or pooled Postgres URL
- connection works
- SSL configuration
- safe read query

==================================================
PHASE 4 — CANONICAL VARIABLE MAPPING
==================================================

Create one server-side variable resolver.

Requirements:

- one canonical name per credential
- temporary aliases supported only for backward compatibility
- startup validation
- clear missing-variable errors
- no secret logging
- production fails safely for required missing server variables
- optional integrations report DISABLED rather than crashing
- public-safe variables separated from server secrets

Example internal mapping:

GitHub:
- canonical: GITHUB_TOKEN
- aliases: GH_TOKEN

Supabase:
- canonical server URL: SUPABASE_URL
- canonical service key: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
- canonical client URL: EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
- canonical client anon key: EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY

AI:
- provider selected explicitly
- credential variable tied to that provider
- base URL tied to that provider
- model validated at startup

Do not automatically copy every secret into every environment.

==================================================
PHASE 5 — FIX OWNER AI TASK ROUTING
==================================================

When the authenticated owner submits a software-development task, route it to the senior-developer runtime.

Required conditions:

- owner authenticated
- task classified as development
- developer tools available
- repository configured
- execution policy allows task

Required behavior:

1. Create task ID.
2. Create trace ID.
3. Persist task.
4. Start Live Work stream.
5. Inspect repository.
6. Run tools.
7. Return actual evidence.

The AI must not answer:

- “I cannot execute”
- “I can guide you”
- “choose one segment”
- “this is too comprehensive”

unless a real tool or authorization failure occurred.

When blocked, return:

- exact blocked stage
- exact tool
- exact runtime error
- HTTP/error code
- trace ID
- specific owner action required

==================================================
PHASE 6 — FIX LIVE WORK
==================================================

Connect the Live Work panel to real persisted backend events.

Required states:

RECEIVED
AUTHORIZED
VARIABLES_VALIDATING
VARIABLES_VALIDATED
INSPECTING
PATCHING
TESTING
COMMITTING
PUSHING
DEPLOYING
VERIFYING
VERIFIED
FAILED
BLOCKED

Each event must contain:

- task ID
- trace ID
- timestamp
- state
- current action
- safe result
- error code
- retryability

The current “Still working” UI must not remain indefinitely.

Add:

- timeout
- cancellation
- reconnect
- persisted progress
- retry
- explicit failure result

==================================================
PHASE 7 — CONTROLLED REAL EXECUTION TEST
==================================================

After repairing routing and variables, execute one safe test.

Task:

Add a safe owner-only diagnostic field to the existing protected developer health endpoint:

"seniorDeveloperRuntime": {
  "enabled": true,
  "variablesValidated": true,
  "toolRegistryReady": true,
  "commitSha": "<runtime SHA>",
  "checkedAt": "<timestamp>"
}

Restrictions:

- no CRM data changes
- no member changes
- no investor changes
- no buyer changes
- no payment changes
- no auth-account changes
- no secret values
- no destructive migration

Required execution:

1. Inspect files.
2. Change code.
3. Add test.
4. Run lint.
5. Run typecheck.
6. Run unit test.
7. Run integration test.
8. Commit.
9. Push.
10. Deploy.
11. Verify live endpoint.
12. Send one Owner AI message after deployment.

==================================================
PHASE 8 — REQUIRED COMMAND EVIDENCE
==================================================

For every command return:

- exact command
- working directory
- start time
- finish time
- exit code
- result

No exit code means the command is NOT VERIFIED.

==================================================
PHASE 9 — DEPLOYMENT VERIFICATION
==================================================

Return:

- GitHub repository
- branch
- commit SHA
- remote SHA
- Render service
- Render deployment ID
- deployed SHA
- boot time
- /health status
- /readiness status
- protected diagnostic status
- Owner AI message ID
- response ID
- trace ID

Confirm:

GitHub SHA = Render deployed SHA = runtime health SHA

==================================================
PHASE 10 — DATA SAFETY
==================================================

Before and after the controlled test record:

- members count
- investors count
- buyers count
- deals count

Expected change:

ZERO

No record may be deleted, modified, merged or restored during this senior-developer runtime test.

==================================================
MANDATORY FINAL REPORT
==================================================

TASK UNDERSTOOD:
[exact task]

ORIGINAL FAILURE:
- User message:
- Route selected:
- Runtime selected:
- Why execution did not start:
- Trace ID:

PIPELINE RESULTS:
- Owner authentication:
- Classification:
- Developer runtime:
- Tool registry:
- Variable resolver:
- Repository:
- Command runner:
- GitHub:
- Render:
- Live Work:

VARIABLE INVENTORY:
1. Variable:
   Service:
   Present:
   Runtime loaded:
   Safe validation:
   Result:
2.

CREDENTIAL TESTS:
- GitHub:
- Render:
- Supabase:
- AI provider:
- Expo/EAS:
- Storage:
- Notifications:
- Redis:
- Database:

Important:
Never show secret values.

FILES INSPECTED:
-
-

FILES CHANGED:
-
-

COMMANDS RUN:
1. Command:
   Exit code:
   Result:

TEST RESULTS:
- Lint:
- Typecheck:
- Unit:
- Integration:
- Owner AI routing:
- Live Work:
- Timeout/cancel:
- Variable startup validation:

GITHUB PROOF:
- Repository:
- Branch:
- Commit SHA:
- Remote SHA:
- Push timestamp:

DEPLOYMENT PROOF:
- Render service:
- Deploy ID:
- Deployed SHA:
- Health:
- Readiness:
- Boot time:

LIVE OWNER AI PROOF:
- Task ID:
- Message ID:
- Response ID:
- Trace ID:
- Execution states:
- Final result:

DATA-SAFETY PROOF:
- Members before:
- Members after:
- Investors before:
- Investors after:
- Buyers before:
- Buyers after:
- Deals before:
- Deals after:
- Business-data changes: ZERO

UNAVAILABLE OR INVALID CREDENTIALS:
- NONE, or:
  - variable
  - exact runtime result
  - HTTP status
  - required owner action

REMAINING ISSUES:
- NONE or exact issue

FINAL STATUS:
VERIFIED / FAILED / BLOCKED

VERIFIED RULE:
Do not use VERIFIED unless:
- all required runtime variables were safely validated
- Owner AI routes a real developer task to the execution worker
- repository inspection occurred
- commands ran with exit codes
- a real code diff exists
- GitHub push succeeded
- Render deployment ID exists
- deployed SHA matches GitHub
- live diagnostic works
- Owner AI returns execution evidence
- business-data counts remain unchanged
